goal: Restore the agent spawning bridge — bring back full ceremony, real agent dispatch via Agent tool, all worker castes (Archaeologist, Oracle, Architect, Ambassador, Builder waves, Watcher, Measurer, Chaos, Scout), depth prompts, named workers with caste colors, graveyard/midden system, QUEEN.md wisdom pipeline, hive brain, skill injection, cross-agent context flow, tiered escalation, and visually rich output with emojis and caste identity. The Go runtime manages state and produces structured dispatch plans; the markdown wrappers must read those plans and spawn real workers. Everything that made v5.4 feel alive, rebuilt on top of the current Go runtime.

phases.name: Assumptions and gap audit
phases.description: Verify the ceremony revival assumptions and lock the wrapper-vs-runtime contract before deeper implementation.

tasks.goal: Verify Node, embed, event bus, ANSI, and v5.4 playbook assumptions
constraints: Node must remain a soft dependency
Do not claim cross-process event streaming from the in-process bus
success_criteria: Assumptions are documented with code references
Incorrect assumptions are corrected before implementation

tasks.goal: Map current build, continue, and plan wrappers against the v5.4 direct-spawn playbooks
constraints: Go remains state source of truth
Wrappers own platform Task-tool spawning where available
success_criteria: Every hollow ceremony gap has an owner
The bridge contract is explicit for Claude, OpenCode, and Codex

tasks.goal: Persist the blended v1.6 ceremony plan for resume and review
constraints: Keep rollback boundaries clear
success_criteria: The active colony plan reflects TS narrator plus real subagent spawning
Resume context points at the correct next slice
The colony has the correct blended roadmap
No implementation starts from a false architecture premise

phases.name: Event protocol and narrator foundations
phases.description: Bundle the TypeScript narrator and expose the Go event and visual contracts it consumes.

tasks.goal: Add the bundled .aether/ts narrator package and embed it in install/update assets
constraints: Go-only installs must keep working without Node
Dependencies must be pinned and auditable
success_criteria: aether install syncs the narrator package
The narrator typechecks under Node 18-compatible tooling

tasks.goal: Define ceremony event topics and a streamable NDJSON event surface
constraints: Cross-process streaming must use persisted events or parent piping
Existing query/publish commands must keep their envelope contract
success_criteria: ceremony.* events can be streamed as raw NDJSON
Existing event bus tests remain green

tasks.goal: Export caste visual identity from Go for narrator consumption
constraints: Do not duplicate emoji/color maps in TypeScript
success_criteria: visuals-dump exposes caste emoji, color, and label metadata
Narrator can consume Go-owned identity data
The narrator package is shipped
Go exposes stable ceremony and visual contracts

phases.name: Rolling activity display
phases.description: Recreate the v5.4 colony activity frame on top of ceremony events.

tasks.goal: Maintain a narrator frame keyed by wave and spawn ID
constraints: Avoid flickery output
Strip ANSI when transcripts need clean text
success_criteria: Active, completed, blocked, and failed workers render distinctly
Progress bars derive from event counts

tasks.goal: Render tool counts, blockers, files, tokens when available, and pheromone/skill activations
constraints: Do not invent token counts when workers do not report them
success_criteria: Narrator output is rich but evidence-backed
Missing fields degrade cleanly
Build activity is visibly alive without lying about worker work

phases.name: Subagent spawn restoration
phases.description: Restore real Claude/OpenCode agent spawning from the Go runtime manifest and v5.4 playbooks.

tasks.goal: Restore build wrappers as Task-tool orchestrators over Go-owned manifests
constraints: Wrappers must parse structured runtime output
Go must remain source of state truth
success_criteria: Builder waves spawn real platform agents
spawn-log and spawn-complete surround every Task call

constraints: Specialists must be conditional and evidence-backed
success_criteria: Archaeology, Oracle, Architect, Ambassador, Probe, Watcher, Measurer, and Chaos all fire in the right places
Cross-agent findings flow into downstream prompts
/ant-build performs construction, not theater
Worker artifacts and spawn tree reflect real work

phases.name: Continue and plan orchestration
phases.description: Bring back depth prompts, Scout plus Route-Setter planning, and verification-led advancement.

tasks.goal: Restore plan depth ceremony and iterative Scout/Route-Setter planning
constraints: Planning must remain bounded by selected depth
Stall detection must stop loops
success_criteria: Users can choose Fast, Balanced, Deep, or Exhaustive planning
Plans are grounded by real Scout and Route-Setter outputs

tasks.goal: Restore continue verification gates before phase advancement
constraints: No advancement on unverified or fake worker claims
success_criteria: Watcher, Auditor, Gatekeeper, and Probe gates run honestly
Midden and blockers update from real failures
Planning and continue are real orchestration surfaces again

phases.name: Full lifecycle ceremony and skills
phases.description: Extend ceremony through colonize, pheromones, graves, chamber sealing, skills, QUEEN.md, and Hive context.

tasks.goal: Add narrator ceremonies for colonize, plan, build, continue, pheromones, graves, and sealing
constraints: Ceremony must follow actual state transitions
success_criteria: Core lifecycle commands emit ceremony events
Graveyard and chamber rituals are visible and state-backed

tasks.goal: Surface skills, QUEEN.md, Hive, midden, and pheromones in worker context and narration
constraints: Visible context must be the same context workers receive
success_criteria: Skill activation and steering context are visible
Narration does not drift from prompt injection
The whole colony lifecycle feels alive because real context is moving

phases.name: Parity verification and release
phases.description: Verify the revived ceremony across Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, then prepare v1.6 release rollback paths.

tasks.goal: Run cross-platform lifecycle smoke tests and compare ceremony output
constraints: Platform differences must be explicit and documented
success_criteria: All three supported surfaces show aligned ceremony
No platform reports fake worker progress

tasks.goal: Verify graceful degradation, update sync, docs, and rollback controls
constraints: AETHER_NARRATOR=off must instantly return to terse output
success_criteria: Missing Node does not crash commands
aether update --force syncs .aether/ts
Rollback is documented and tested
v1.6 can ship with known rollback levers
The revived ceremony is verified, not aspirational

decisions.claim: Go owns state/events/contracts, TypeScript owns narration, wrappers own platform Task-tool spawning.
decisions.rationale: Keeps the Go runtime authoritative while restoring v5.4 ceremony and real agent spawning without claiming Go performed platform-only spawns.

decisions.claim: Do not wire runtime launch to the TypeScript narrator until installed dependencies are explicit or dependency-free.
decisions.rationale: The scaffold currently depends on dev tooling; Go-only installs must degrade cleanly and must not require undocumented npm install steps.
